verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (hK2) is a secreted trypsin-like serine protease of the kallikrein family, produced by prostate epithelium and released into seminal fluid / circulation where it processes pro-PSA. It has no TM domain, no GPI anchor, and no documented stable wash-resistant tethering to a cell-surface partner. Walking the contextual buckets: no documented cancer/cell-death/activation-induced ecto-pool of the protein body; no germline/developmental surface display with its own anchor; not a lysosomal TM protein; no documented PM minority pool or cycling; no covalent/wash-resistant TM-partner anchoring. Clinical programs (e.g., radioligands, T-cell engagers) target hK2 protein in prostate tumor microenvironment as a secreted/pericellular antigen, not as a membrane-integral protein, and pMHC-style targeting doesn't credit surface accessibility.
